description: Implement save/load persistence using browser localStorage. Save the complete game state on every sector transition and on quit. Load restores exactly where the player left off. No save-scumming — only one save slot that is deleted on death.
acceptance_criteria: The full game state (player stats, inventory, map explored state, enemy positions, resource levels, alert level, stash contents, ship repair progress) is serializable to JSON.
Game state auto-saves to localStorage when the player transitions between sectors and when they quit to menu.
Given a save exists, when the player opens the game, then a 'Continue' option loads the saved state exactly as it was.
Given the player dies, when the run ends, then the active save is deleted (only the meta-progression data — stash and ship repairs — persists).
If localStorage is full or unavailable, a warning message displays and the game continues without saving.
A 'New Game' option always starts a fresh run (with meta-progression intact).
notes: Spec: 'Save-and-quit (standard roguelike — no save-scumming, but graceful session handling).' localStorage has a ~5MB limit per origin — game state JSON should be well under this. Consider using JSON.stringify with replacer to exclude transient data.
